title: Gastrolobium heterophyllum
intro: Gastrolobium heterophyllum, also known as poison, is a toxic plant species native to Western Australia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Bioactive constituents of Gastrolobium heterophyllum include a range of alkaloids, including heterophylline, which is thought to be responsible for the plant's toxic and medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas and extracts, although extreme caution is advised due to its toxicity and potential for adverse effects.
taxonomy: Gastrolobium heterophyllum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. This plant is native to Australia and is a part of the pea family, which includes a wide range of plants with similar characteristics. The Fabaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse plant families, consisting of over 19,000 known species. As a member of this family, Gastrolobium heterophyllum shares many characteristics with other legumes, such as the presence of compound leaves and pea-like flowers. The classification of Gastrolobium heterophyllum within the Fabaceae family highlights its unique characteristics and relationships with other plants in this family.
uses: Gastrolobium heterophyllum, also known as poison pea or variable Gastrolobium, has various potential medicinal uses. It is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in treating wounds and infections. The plant's extract is also reported to have antioxidant properties, which could help protect against cell damage and oxidative stress. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal potential and its effects on human health. Further studies are required to explore its safety and efficacy as a medicinal agent.

preparations: Gastrolobium heterophyllum, also known as the "Stinking Gillespie", is a plant species native to Western Australia. It has been used by the local indigenous people to create herbal preparations for various purposes.
